title: EDGAR ↔ GLEIF — company-to-LEI crosswalk
description: A resolved crosswalk linking SEC EDGAR registrants (CIK / ticker / fund series) to their GLEIF Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) — one row per asserted identity, each carrying its provenance tier, method and confidence. Authoritative edges come from official SEC/GLEIF registration (no guessing); the operating-company tail is linked by probabilistic name resolution (Fellegi-Sunter / Splink): exact normalized-name matches are confirmed (~95% precision), while name-only fuzzy matches are surfaced as candidates rather than auto-confirmed. A point-in-time snapshot, with an as-of date on every row.

key_type.description: Scheme of the SEC identifier held in `key`: cik (operating company / registrant), series (fund series) or class (fund share class).

key.description: The SEC-side identifier this edge is keyed on — an EDGAR CIK for operating companies / registrants, or an SEC series/class id for funds, per `key_type`.

category.description: GLEIF's authoritative entity category (e.g. GENERAL, FUND, BRANCH); GENERAL when unknown.

method.description: How the edge was established. Authoritative: sec-registration (GLEIF RA000665) or sec-ncen (SEC Form N-CEN). Probabilistic: exact_name (identical normalized company name) or jaro_winkler (fuzzy name).

tier.description: Provenance tier, in precedence order: authoritative (deterministic SEC/GLEIF registration — no guessing), confirmed (exact normalized-name Splink match, ~95% precision), candidate (name-only fuzzy match, surfaced NOT asserted), ambiguous (one exact name matched more than one entity). A deterministic edge always wins over a name match for the same key.

confidence.description: Edge confidence in [0, 1]: 1.0 for authoritative edges; the Fellegi-Sunter posterior match probability under the frozen resolution model for probabilistic edges.

corpus.description: Lowercased, accent-stripped concatenation of the display name, legal name, ticker and LEI — a derived free-text search field, not a source attribute.

foreignKeys.x-evidence: For key_type='cik' rows, `key` is the EDGAR Central Index Key exactly as reported by SEC EDGAR — the crosswalk is keyed on EDGAR's own CIK, so this edge is exact by construction. (series/class rows key on an SEC fund identifier and do not reference EDGAR.)

x-evidence.method: Fellegi-Sunter probabilistic record linkage (Splink), frozen model, for the operating-company tail; official SEC/GLEIF registration for authoritative edges
x-evidence.comparison: normalized company name (exact, then Jaro-Winkler tiers)
x-evidence.tie_break: ISSUED > LAPSED > RETIRED, then US jurisdiction
x-evidence.precision: exact normalized-name matches auto-confirmed (~95% precision, spot-checked); name-only fuzzy matches surfaced as candidate, not confirmed
